# Copilot Instructions — lwc-tailwind

## Project overview

This is a Salesforce CLI plugin (`sf` CLI) that enables Tailwind CSS in Lightning Web Components (LWC). It works around Shadow DOM CSS isolation by:

1. Extracting `--tw-*` base variables into a shared static resource (loaded via `loadStyle`)
2. Splitting utility class rules per-component (only the classes each LWC actually uses)

## Tech stack

- TypeScript (ES2022, Node16 modules)
- oclif (Salesforce CLI plugin framework)
- PostCSS (CSS compilation)
- chokidar (file watching)

## Project structure

- `src/commands/tailwind/` — CLI commands: init, build, watch, component
- `src/services/css-builder.ts` — Core CSS parsing and splitting logic
- `src/services/project.ts` — Reads sfdx-project.json, resolves paths
- `src/templates/` — Code templates for generated files
- `messages/` — Command help text (oclif markdown format)
- `example/` — Sample Salesforce project for testing

## Build pipeline

```
src/tailwind.css → (PostCSS) → src/.tailwind-compiled.css → (parseTailwindCss) →
  ├─ staticresources/tailwind.css  (base --tw-* variables only)
  └─ lwc/<comp>/<comp>.css         (matched utility rules only)
```

## Key patterns

- Components extend `TailwindElement` (not `LightningElement`)
- Component CSS files use a marker: `/* === GENERATED BY TAILWIND SPLITTER === */`
- Hand-written CSS above the marker is preserved; generated rules go below
- Class extraction scans both `.html` (class attributes) and `.js` (string literals)
- `isBaseRule()` identifies base rules: no class selectors + all declarations are `--tw-*`

## Development workflow

After any TypeScript change:
```bash
npm run build        # Compile TS → lib/
sf plugins link .    # Re-link for testing
cd example && sf tailwind build  # Test
```

The `sf` CLI uses compiled JS from `lib/`. Forgetting to rebuild causes stale code to run.

## Conventions

- LWC component names must be camelCase (e.g., `myButton`)
- The intermediate file `src/.tailwind-compiled.css` should never be committed or deployed
- Static resource contains ONLY base variables (~105 lines), not utility classes
